method_atom: A technique for deriving a closed system of nonlinear associativity (consistency) equations for a single generating potential by taking the long-wavelength / semiclassical (dispersionless) limit of the bilinear consistency identities of an integrable hierarchy. In this limit any generating function of the limiting hierarchy automatically yields a solution of the associativity system, and particular solutions can be constructed from a correspondence with conformal mappings, giving families parametrized by finitely or infinitely many variables.
object_atom: The Witten-Dijkgraaf-Verlinde-Verlinde (WDVV) associativity equations and the dispersionless (quasiclassical / Whitham) KP and Toda integrable hierarchies studied in mathematical physics and topological field theory.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the machinery of extracting associativity/consistency equations for a generating potential from the semiclassical limit of an integrable system's bilinear (Hirota) identities; the specific WDVV equations and dispersionless KP/Toda hierarchies are the domain objects, somewhat fused since these associativity equations are here defined via those particular hierarchies.
